name: Drago's Eggplant Souffle in Tomato-Basil Sauc
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel eggplants and cut into cubes. Set aside.
2. Cut zucchini into quarters and remove centers. Discard the removed centers. Cut remaining zucchini into cubes. Repeat process with pepper and set aside.
3. Heat olive oil in a sauté pan. Add eggplant, potato, onion, basil, oregano, and salt. Sauté until golden. Add tomatoes and cook for about 10 minutes.
4. Transfer the mixture to a food processor and blend until smooth. Add Parmesan cheese, bread crumbs, and eggs. Pulse in the processor for 5 seconds.
5. Coat the bottom of 8 (6-ounce) timbals with butter. Divide zucchini and pepper pieces evenly among the cups. Fill the rest of each cup with the eggplant mixture.
6. Bake in a bain-marie at 350°F (175°C) for 40 minutes.
7. Remove from oven and let stand for 15 minutes.
8. When ready to serve, spoon a small amount of Tomato-Basil Sauce onto individual serving plates. Place the souffle cups on top of each plate. Serve warm. Makes 8 servings.
Drago serves these individual souffles as appetizers at his restaurant. It's a wonderful use of eggplant, one of the most commonly used vegetables in Sicily. Using good Parmesan cheese is essential, as it rounds out the flavor.
